mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound compresses the equity discount rate and incentivizes risk-taking, producing persistent positive S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains below 1%. This is an always-on carry effect independent of short-term news flow.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.15 at snapshot, near zero bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window when FEDFUNDS < 1%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate declines by more than 0.3 percentage points month-over-month from an elevated crisis level (above 6%), it signals an accelerating labor market recovery that boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.3pp month-over-month while UNRATE level > 6%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=7.6 at snapshot, elevated post-shock level
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns following triggered UNRATE declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two distinct recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflects a self-reinforcing consumption-employment feedback loop that is not captured by either series alone; simultaneous positive growth in both signals a durable demand expansion driving S&P 500 gains over the next quarter.
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery with rising consumption and employment
failure_condition: The joint-trigger sample shows forward 60d hit rate for positive SPX returns no better than the unconditional base rate across a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) during a near-zero federal funds rate regime signal interest-rate-sensitive demand recovery that propagates through construction, durable goods, and consumer confidence channels, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is regime-specific because low-rate financing amplifies housing responsiveness.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, positive HOUST momentum fails to produce forward 20d SPX returns above median baseline in more than 50% of rolling 6-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Short-horizon momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving manufacturing throughput that is quickly priced into equities; a positive month-over-month change in INDPRO predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as markets react to the incremental supply-side signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (measured via CPIAUCSL year-over-year change) crosses above 3% while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, markets begin pricing in future rate hikes, raising the equity discount rate and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as tightening expectations build.
regime_hint: early inflation acceleration under near-zero rates
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns following the trigger are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) or the sign is reversed in more than half of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points is a threshold labor-market improvement signal that leads risk assets higher over the following month as investors reprice growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Hit rate of positive forward 20d SPX returns after triggered UNRATE declines is not significantly above 55% across a 3-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of a near-zero nominal federal funds rate with positive CPI inflation implies deeply negative real interest rates, which historically compress bond yields relative to equity earnings yield and drive capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. Neither series alone captures this real-rate channel.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.15 at snapshot suggests deeply negative real rates if inflation positive
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns conditioned on real rate < -1% are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) across at least two distinct historical episodes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
